channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The best ways to give your old Android a second life
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how you can creatively repurpose your old unused Android devices, which are full of potential and hidden value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World’s first AI-powered industrial super-humanoid robot
item.description: Mech the super-humanoid robot can lift up to 132 pounds and is designed to tackle stressful and repetitive tasks that often lead to workplace injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Google Assistant bug seems to be causing some users to miss alarms and calls on Android
item.description: Users are reporting that Do Not Disturb isn't correctly set through Google Assistant on Android phones.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At 50, Microsoft highlights AI and Copilot as the company’s future
item.description: At a special 50th anniversary event on Friday, Microsoft executives reflected on the company’s storied past and on how it’s now reinventing itself for an AI-focused future.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: A man appearing before a New York court got a scolding from a judge after he tried to use an avatar generated by artificial intelligence to argue his case.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Intel could be key to Nvidia's future plans for megawatt-class rack servers as it pushes ahead with Superfluid cooling
item.description: Intel’s Superfluid cooling solution is capable of handling 1.5kW of heat and could be used on Nvidia's GB300 superchip.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How to remotely reset your router: Steps for easy troubleshooting
item.description: You can reset your internet router remotely if you're not home when your power goes out. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how this can be done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fires NSA director and deputy after Loomer meeting
item.description: The Trump administration has fired the director of the National Security Agency as well as his deputy, prompting outrage from congressional Democrats. Director Gen.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Increasingly dangerous scam targets kids through sextortion
item.description: The FBI issued a report that described sextortion as “a growing threat,” noting that the agency investigated roughly 1,000 cases a month for much of 2021 through early 2023.
item.dc:creator: Andre Mouchard, The Mercury News

item.title: High-tech canopy provides off-grid power and water
item.description: The EO Canopy is a self-sustaining, solar-powered camping platform designed to provide all the comforts of home while completely off the grid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Trump's tariffs are so bad for Big Tech
item.description: President Trump’s sweeping new slate of tariffs is poised to strike a blow at the tech industry, as massive import taxes on China and Taiwan disrupt trade flows that are central to tech firms’ business models.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Top 15 Microsoft Azure certifications to expedite your IT career
item.description: With companies increasingly moving their data to cloud, there’s an extensive need for more professionals with sound understanding and expertise of cloud technology. Microsoft’s cloud offering, Azure, ranks among the top in the industry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cobas Asset Management moderniza su atención al cliente con un asistente virtual impulsado por IA
item.description: Cobas Asset Management ha puesto en marcha un nuevo proyecto con vistas a mejorar la experiencia de usuario en su web y en su área privada gracias a la incorporación de un asistente virtual.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Blissfully fast and simple JavaScript frameworks
item.description: Simple and fast. That’s all we want from our software tools. And meeting the requirements, of course. You can’t go without that. Also, we’d like them to be pleasant to use, with a good developer experience. It would be really great to have a friendly community.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Explorando la confluencia de la tecnología y la sostenibilidad para un futuro mejor
item.description: Los esfuerzos de sostenibilidad están cobrando fuerza a escala mundial, y los gobiernos y las organizaciones están poniendo todo su empeño en la formulación y aplicación de políticas que puedan detener o incluso revertir el daño medioambiental global y crear un impacto positivo para las comunidades
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: From ensuring the quality of data to fostering a culture of experimentation and adaptability, leaders must actively steer AI’s integration into operations. The post Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: New Relic simplifies Kubernetes performance monitoring
item.description: New Relic this week announced a new capability in its Intelligent Observability Platform that the company says will enable engineering teams to deploy an eBPF-powered agent to automatically discover and monitor all applications running on Kubernetes clusters.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Accelerate cloud infrastructure initiatives with Lucid Software
item.description: Cloud and development operations (devops) teams are under constant pressure to do more, faster. They have outdated systems to update and manage, new systems to build, and requests from business units who need their support. It’s a velocity problem, and IT departments often struggle to keep pace.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: United receives FAA certification on Starlink-equipped aircraft
item.description: United has received FAA approval for a Starlink-equipped plane. The first commercial flight is set for May.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, The Hill

item.title: Potential Nvidia chip shortage looms as Chinese customers rush to beat US sales ban
item.description: The AI chip shortage could become even more dire as Chinese customers are purportedly looking to hoard Nvidia chips ahead of a proposed US sales ban.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Django 5.2 release touts automatic model importing
item.description: The latest version of the Python web framework features an automatic model-importing capability in the shell. This release also spells the end of support levels for previous version 5.x releases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Mozilla unveils its Gmail challenger
item.description: Mozilla has decided to launch a challenger to Gmail called Thundermail. The upstart is a web-based email service based on the open-source project Stalwart, according to technology site Thurrott.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to hold a meeting on possible investors to buy TikTok with possible ban at stake
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump will hold a Wednesday meeting with aides about possible investors who could buy a stake in TikTok, a deal that could potentially stop the social media site from being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At the VA, modernizing healthcare delivery rests in patient’s control of their data
item.description: Neil Evans, acting program executive director for electronic health record modernization at VA said the VA has always had some form of electronic health record. The post At the VA, modernizing healthcare delivery rests in patient’s control of their data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Dow wants to power its Texas manufacturing complex with new nuclear reactors instead of natural gas
item.description: Dow, a major producer of chemicals and plastics, wants to use next-generation nuclear reactors for clean power and steam at a Texas manufacturing complex instead of natural gas. Dow's subsidiary, Long Mott Energy, applied Monday to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission for a construction permit.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jack Black, Jason Momoa enter the Minecraft universe as the video game phenom hits the big screen
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When Jack Black got the call to star in the “Minecraft” movie, the perks were plenty. He got to work alongside Jason Momoa, reunite with “Nacho Libre” director Jared Hess and engulf himself into a beloved gaming universe.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: China's telecoms giant Huawei Technologies says its profit fell 28% while revenue surged in 2024
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Chinese telecoms gear giant Huawei said Monday its net profit fell 28% in 2024 while its revenue surged, as the company invested heavily in advanced technologies.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5
